summary.intent: Monitor PR #89 for review comments and CI failures using the pr-watch skill, respond to reviewer feedback by fixing code and resolving threads, then continue monitoring for new activity.
summary.outcome: Completed monitoring cycle 1 (replied to 19 comments, resolved 25 threads, committed 1 fix for follow-up comment detection) and cycle 2 (fixed 2 new copilot review comments about SKILL.md documentation accuracy, committed and pushed). CI failures were identified as GitHub billing issues, not code problems.

repo: All reviewers on this PR (copilot-pull-request-reviewer, devin-ai-integration) are Bots (type: 'Bot'), so re-requesting review via gh CLI is skipped.
CI failures 'Lint Commit Messages' and 'Validate Plugin' were caused by GitHub account billing problems, not code issues — tracked as UNFIXABLE_RUNS=[24329065436, 24329065437].
The pr-watch skill defines UNFIXABLE_RUNS to skip known non-fixable CI run IDs in subsequent cycles.
commitlint scope-enum for this repo: caffeine, catch-up, claude, dev, git, hookify.

code.finding: Monitoring parameters table: idle timeout description needed to include PR_CONFLICT as an activity-resetting event, not just review/CI events.
TIMEOUT_ABS event description was missing the API error consecutive failure case (3 consecutive API errors also trigger TIMEOUT_ABS, not just the 60-minute wall clock limit).

workflow: When context is compacted mid-session, state variables (HAD_ACTIVITY, UNFIXABLE_RUNS, CYCLE_COUNT, etc.) must be passed explicitly in the user message to resume the monitoring loop correctly.
ScheduleWakeup is used instead of sleep for inter-cycle delays since blocking sleep commands are rejected in this environment.
Bot reviewers should be detected before attempting re-request-review to avoid unnecessary API calls.
Review comment threads should be replied to + reacted to + resolved in bulk rather than one-by-one to reduce API round trips.

friction: sleep command was blocked by the environment, requiring a switch to ScheduleWakeup for inter-cycle delays.
Context compaction mid-session lost all in-memory state; required explicit state variable passing in the continuation user message.
CI failures due to GitHub billing issues are indistinguishable from real code failures without investigating run logs — required an Agent subagent to diagnose.
New HEAD SHA detected at cycle 2 start (new commit from cycle 1 push triggered new CI runs), meaning UNFIXABLE_RUN IDs changed and had to be re-identified.
open_items: CI failures ('Lint Commit Messages', 'Validate Plugin') remain unresolved due to GitHub billing issues — not actionable from the codebase side.
Monitoring cycle 2 commit was pushed but the transcript ends before confirming cycle 3 scheduling or PR merge.
No re-review was requested after cycle 2 fixes since all reviewers are Bots — unclear if Bots will auto-re-review on new commits.
PR #89 merge status and final outcome not captured in this transcript.
